operating_state: Total production increased 8% to 1,434 Mboe/d driven by CrownRock acquisition, with Permian Basin production rising 18%. Operating cash flow from continuing operations decreased to $9.6 billion from $10.5 billion due to higher working capital usage from increased tax payments and payables timing. Oil realized prices declined 14% to $64.60/Bbl while domestic natural gas prices increased 68%, resulting in revenue decreasing to $21.6 billion despite higher volumes.
cost_structure: Average lease operating expense per Boe improved to $8.94 from $9.75 driven by Permian operational efficiencies and lower Oman energy costs. Capital expenditures increased to $6.4 billion with Permian development receiving primary allocation, while depreciation increased to $7.5 billion reflecting higher CrownRock production volumes. April 2025 U.S. tariff policy imposing 10% base rates created supplier cost pressures managed through operational efficiencies.
material_events: Completed OxyChem sale to Berkshire Hathaway for $9.7 billion generating estimated $3.2 billion after-tax gain, with March 2026 closing expected subject to regulatory approvals. Used proceeds to repay $5.4 billion debt including full term loan balance, reducing principal debt to $15 billion with additional debt reduction during 2025. Declared common dividends at $0.96 per share with $1.2 billion remaining under share repurchase program.
macro_exposures: Heightened commodity price volatility exposure following OxyChem divestiture, with results correlating to oil, NGL and natural gas pricing driven by global supply-demand dynamics and geopolitical factors. OECD Pillar Two global minimum tax initiatives being enacted across multiple jurisdictions though management expects minimal operational impact. Midland-to-Gulf-Coast oil spreads decreased while Waha-to-Gulf-Coast gas spreads increased.
forward_outlook: Management projects 2026 capital expenditures between $5.5 billion and $5.9 billion with approximately $8.4 billion planned over five years to develop Permian reserves representing 69% of total undeveloped reserves. STRATOS direct air capture facility expected to begin CO2 sequestration in 2026 with Class VI permits secured and five sequestration hubs in development. Post-deleveraging to $14.3 billion debt target, available cash allocated to opportunistic share repurchases and debt reduction.
uncertainty_profile: OxyChem transaction subject to Hart-Scott-Rodino clearance with March 2026 termination rights, while company provided indemnification for pre-closing liabilities and environmental exposures. Tronox settlement tax deductibility pending before U.S. Tax Court with potential $2.3 billion liability if deduction disallowed. Carbon management commercialization subject to market development uncertainties and regulatory support.
